original_hypothesis.title: buy amount=0 allows zero-cost state updates and downstream bloat (ownedCurvesTokenSubjects), enabling gas griefing and storage growth
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: Curves.buyCurvesToken does not enforce amount>0. For supply>0 subjects, getPrice(supply,0) returns 0 and fees are 0, so a caller can execute _buyCurvesToken with msg.value=0, triggering ownership tracking updates and (if holders fees enabled) FeeSplitter hooks. This can be spammed to bloat per-user subject lists and/or FeeSplitter.userTokens via onBalanceChange side effects.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: Zero-cost callable paths are commonly exploited to create unbounded storage growth or to degrade UX via large arrays. This is practical on L2s with cheap gas.
deployment_assumptions: At least one subject has existing supply > 0 (so non-subject callers pass UnauthorizedCurvesTokenSubject gate)
Optionally, feeRedistributor is enabled (holdersFeePercent>0) to amplify bloat into FeeSplitter.userTokens
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: Curves.sol _buyCurvesToken requires supply>0 OR msg.sender==subject; _transferFees only calls feeRedistributor when holdersFeePercent>0 and feeRedistributor!=0.

proof_of_signal.pattern: _buyCurvesToken has no `require(amount > 0)`; updates balances/supply by `+= amount` and may call _addOwnedCurvesTokenSubject when prior balance was 0

preconditions: Attacker can call buyCurvesToken with amount=0 and msg.value=0
If amplifying into FeeSplitter: holdersFeePercent>0 and feeRedistributor set

initial_code_evidence.vulnerable_pattern: missing non-zero amount validation enables free calls
initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: storage bloat via ownership tracking and potential FeeSplitter.userTokens duplication
initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: no min amount; no rate limit; no duplicate checks in downstream arrays

analysis_questions: Does _addOwnedCurvesTokenSubject add entries when amount=0 and prior balance==0? (it will, because condition checks prior balance using `- amount` logic)
Can this be used to bloat ownedCurvesTokenSubjects for the attacker (and later make transferAllCurvesTokens unusable due to gas)?
If feeRedistributor is enabled, does _transferFees call onBalanceChange even when holderFee is 0, causing FeeSplitter.userTokens bloat at zero cost?
validation_requirements: Test buyCurvesToken(subject,0) succeeds with msg.value=0 when supply>0
Check ownedCurvesTokenSubjects[msg.sender] growth and Transfer/Trade events
If feeRedistributor enabled, check FeeSplitter.userTokens growth and batchClaiming gas

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: Attacker repeatedly calls buyCurvesToken(existingSubject,0) to create large per-user arrays, making their own transferAllCurvesTokens expensive/unusable and potentially amplifying FeeSplitter bookkeeping bloat.

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Indirect; increased gas costs and potential functional DoS for affected accounts
potential_impact_if_confirmed.affected_users: Primarily attacker-owned accounts; potentially system-wide if FeeSplitter arrays are amplified and used by UI/batch claims

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: No tests asserting amount>0 on buys or bounding array growth

technical_investigation_focus.gas_analysis_needed: estimate number of spam calls before transferAllCurvesTokens becomes non-viable
technical_investigation_focus.detection_analysis: storage growth visible but may be ignored

validation_result.mechanism: missing zero-amount validation enables free state updates and array bloat
validation_result.impact_summary: buyCurvesToken(subject, 0) succeeds with msg.value=0 for any subject with supply>0, triggering events, external calls, and unbounded duplicate pushes to FeeSplitter.userTokens when holdersFeePercent>0.
validation_result.detailed_analysis: The _buyCurvesToken function lacks a `require(amount > 0)` guard. When amount=0 and supply>0, getPrice(supply, 0) returns 0 because sum2==sum1, yielding price=0 and totalFee=0. The msg.value>=0 check always passes. The function then proceeds to call _transferFees (which sends 0 ETH to fee destinations, emits Trade events, and if holdersFeePercent>0, calls feeRedistributor.onBalanceChange). In onBalanceChange, if the caller already holds tokens for the subject (balanceOf>0), `userTokens[account].push(token)` appends a duplicate entry with no dedup check. This push occurs on every zero-amount buy at zero ETH cost, enabling unbounded growth of userTokens[attacker]. Additionally, if the caller's balance was 0, the condition `curvesTokenBalance - amount == 0` evaluates to `0 - 0 == 0` which is true, triggering _addOwnedCurvesTokenSubject (though this has a dedup check and only adds once per subject). The zero-amount buy is a real code defect: it produces no economic value but triggers state-changing side effects.
validation_result.code_evidence_assessment: Lines 218-233 of Curves.sol show _buyCurvesToken with no amount>0 check. Lines 155-162 of Curves.sol show getPrice returning 0 when sum2==sum1 (amount=0, supply>0). Lines 96-97 of FeeSplitter.sol show onBalanceChange unconditionally pushing to userTokens when balance>0 with no duplicate check. Lines 224-225 of Curves.sol show msg.value check passes trivially when price+totalFee=0.

vulnerable_code_snippets.vulnerability_explanation: No require(amount > 0). With amount=0 and supply>0, getPrice returns 0, all fees are 0, msg.value>=0 always holds. The function proceeds to call _transferFees which triggers onBalanceChange, pushing duplicates to FeeSplitter.userTokens when caller already holds tokens.
When supply>0 and amount=0, sum2 equals sum1 (both compute the same expression with supply-1+0 = supply-1), so summation=0 and price=0.
No duplicate check before push. Every zero-amount buy triggers this when holdersFeePercent>0, adding duplicate entries to userTokens[account] if the account has existing balance>0.

calling_sequence: 2. Presale check passes (startTime == 0 or startTime < block.timestamp)
9. _transferFees called with price=0, sends 0 ETH to destinations
11. onBalanceChange: if caller has existing balance>0, pushes duplicate to userTokens[msg.sender]
13. Back in _buyCurvesToken: if caller balance was 0, adds subject to ownedCurvesTokenSubjects (with dedup)
execution_path_analysis.vulnerable_code_path: The entire path executes successfully with amount=0 and msg.value=0. The key vulnerability is at step 11: onBalanceChange (FeeSplitter.sol:96) pushes token to userTokens[account] without checking for duplicates, and this is triggered on every zero-amount buy. Additionally at step 13, if the caller had 0 balance, the condition (Curves.sol:232) `curvesTokenBalance[subject][msg.sender] - 0 == 0` is true, adding the subject to ownedCurvesTokenSubjects (but _addOwnedCurvesTokenSubject has dedup so only adds once per subject).

deployment_assumptions_checked: At least one subject has existing supply > 0
holdersFeePercent > 0 and feeRedistributor set for FeeSplitter amplification
config_validation.deployment_evidence: Deployment scripts (local-setup.ts) call deployContracts() and postDeploy() from external config not provided. The Curves constructor takes feeRedistributor_ as parameter, confirming it is intended to be set. Fee percentages are set post-deployment via setter functions. Scripts don't show exact fee configuration values.

validation_result.attack_scenario_validation: Attack scenario is valid: (1) Attacker buys 1 token of existingSubject (paying minimal price for supply=1). (2) Attacker repeatedly calls buyCurvesToken(existingSubject, 0) with msg.value=0. Each call costs only gas. (3) If holdersFeePercent>0 and feeRedistributor is set, each call pushes a duplicate to userTokens[attacker] in FeeSplitter. After thousands of calls, getUserTokensAndClaimable and batchClaiming become unusable due to gas limits. (4) For ownedCurvesTokenSubjects bloat: attacker calls buyCurvesToken(subjectN, 0) for many different subjects with supply>0. Each adds a new entry (one per subject, dedup prevents duplicates). This makes transferAllCurvesTokens expensive. On L2s with cheap gas, thousands of spam calls are economically feasible.
validation_result.impact_assessment: MEDIUM severity confirmed. Primary impact: (1) Unbounded growth of FeeSplitter.userTokens[attacker] via duplicate pushes — makes batchClaiming/getUserTokensAndClaimable fail with OOG for the attacker's address. (2) Growth of ownedCurvesTokenSubjects[attacker] across many subjects at zero ETH cost — makes transferAllCurvesTokens fail with OOG. (3) Wasted storage and event emissions. The DoS is primarily self-inflicted (attacker's own arrays), but there are scenarios where this matters: e.g., a contract wallet whose transfer functionality relies on transferAllCurvesTokens, or UI that calls getUserTokensAndClaimable for display. Additionally, the onBalanceChange call resets the attacker's own fee offset, which is a separate issue (INV-STATE-001).
recommended_mitigations: Add `require(amount > 0)` or `if (amount == 0) revert()` at the start of _buyCurvesToken
Add duplicate check in FeeSplitter.onBalanceChange before pushing to userTokens (defense in depth)
Consider adding a mechanism to remove entries from ownedCurvesTokenSubjects when balance reaches 0
validation_result.replication_plan: 1. Deploy Curves with FeeSplitter, set holdersFeePercent>0. 2. Subject A buys 1 token (supply becomes 1). 3. Attacker buys 1 token of Subject A (supply becomes 2, attacker balance=1). 4. Attacker calls buyCurvesToken(subjectA, 0) with msg.value=0 — expect success. 5. Check FeeSplitter.getUserTokens(attacker) — expect duplicate entry. 6. Repeat step 4 1000 times, check array length. 7. Call batchClaiming with getUserTokens result — expect OOG.
uncertainties: Whether protocolFeeDestination.call{value:0}('') succeeds when protocolFeeDestination is address(0) — depends on EVM behavior with precompile at address 0
Exact gas cost per zero-amount buy on target L2 chains — determines practical spam scale
Whether any UI or contract integration relies on getUserTokensAndClaimable for other users (would amplify impact beyond self-DoS)
additional_testing_needed: Test buyCurvesToken(subject, 0) with msg.value=0 when protocolFeeDestination is set to a valid address vs address(0)
Measure gas growth of getUserTokensAndClaimable and batchClaiming as userTokens array grows
Test transferAllCurvesTokens gas consumption as ownedCurvesTokenSubjects grows with zero-amount buys across many subjects
Verify that sellCurvesToken(subject, 0) also works (supply > 0 check would fail since supply <= 0 is checked)
